params.delta: specs.check: all public functions have @spec or exemption

diff.summary:
diff --git a/elixir/lib/symphony_elixir/game_api/client.ex b/elixir/lib/symphony_elixir/game_api/client.ex
index 906ff9496ab2b0ccf547e666e3c64e097569d7a0..5ef5a147c12f8001ae0208d5c993bb7c18fdc89d
--- a/elixir/lib/symphony_elixir/game_api/client.ex
+++ b/elixir/lib/symphony_elixir/game_api/client.ex
@@ -120,6 +120,35 @@
     end
   end
 
+  @spec fetch_pull_request(String.t(), pos_integer()) :: {:ok, map()} | {:error, term()}
+  def fetch_pull_request(repository_id, pull_request_number)
+      when is_binary(repository_id) and is_integer(pull_request_number) and pull_request_number > 0 do
+    with {:ok, repository} <- find_repository(repository_id) do
+      request(:get, "/v1/internal/bos/delivery/pull-requests/#{pull_request_number}",
+        params: repository_query(repository)
+      )
+    end
+  end
+
+  @spec mark_pull_request_ready(Issue.t(), pos_integer(), String.t()) ::
+          {:ok, map()} | {:error, term()}
+  def mark_pull_request_ready(%Issue{native_ref: native_ref}, pull_request_number, expected_head_sha)
+      when is_integer(pull_request_number) and pull_request_number > 0 and
+             is_binary(expected_head_sha) and expected_head_sha != "" do
+    with {:ok, repository} <- find_repository(native_ref["repositoryId"]) do
+      request(:post, "/v1/internal/bos/delivery/pull-requests/ready",
+        json: %{
+          "repository" => repository_body(repository),
+          "issueNumber" => native_ref["issueNumber"],
+          "operationId" => "pull_request_ready_#{native_ref["runId"]}_#{pull_request_number}",
+          "runId" => native_ref["runId"],
+          "pullRequestNumber" => pull_request_number,
+          "expectedHeadSha" => expected_head_sha
+        }
+      )
+    end
+  end
+
   @spec fetch_run_artifacts(String.t(), String.t(), String.t()) :: {:ok, [map()]} | {:error, term()}
   def fetch_run_artifacts(repository_id, run_id, directory)
       when is_binary(repository_id) and is_binary(run_id) and is_binary(directory) do
diff --git a/elixir/lib/symphony_elixir/post_approval_coordinator.ex b/elixir/lib/symphony_elixir/post_approval_coordinator.ex
index cfe1e3ea4f60acc956706f5e116a1d3bd908f902..c7c692132852c8a00710664af0ab6fe460e40dcd
--- a/elixir/lib/symphony_elixir/post_approval_coordinator.ex
+++ b/elixir/lib/symphony_elixir/post_approval_coordinator.ex
@@ -19,7 +19,8 @@
     app_server = Keyword.get(opts, :app_server_module, AppServer)
     client = Keyword.get(opts, :game_api_client_module, Client)
 
-    with :ok <- require_local_probe_handoff(worker_host),
+    with :ok <- ensure_pull_request_ready(client, issue),
+         :ok <- require_local_probe_handoff(worker_host),
          :ok <- prepare_probe_handoff(workspace),
          :ok <- run_verification_turn(app_server, workspace, issue, recipient, worker_host),
          {:ok, request} <- load_probe_request(workspace, issue),
@@ -30,11 +31,68 @@
     end
   end
 
+  defp ensure_pull_request_ready(client, %Issue{native_ref: native_ref} = issue) do
+    repository_id = native_ref["repositoryId"]
+    run_id = native_ref["runId"]
+
+    with {:ok, run} <- client.fetch_run(repository_id, run_id),
+         {:ok, identity} <- pull_request_identity(run),
+         {:ok, pull_request} <- client.fetch_pull_request(repository_id, identity.number),
+         :ok <- validate_pull_request(pull_request, identity) do
+      if pull_request["draft"] do
+        promote_and_confirm(client, issue, identity)
+      else
+        :ok
+      end
+    end
+  end
+
+  defp pull_request_identity(run) do
+    number = run["pullRequestNumber"]
+    head_sha = run["headCommit"]
+
+    cond do
+      not is_integer(number) or number <= 0 -> {:error, :pull_request_identity_missing}
+      not is_binary(head_sha) or head_sha == "" -> {:error, :pull_request_head_missing}
+      true -> {:ok, %{number: number, head_sha: head_sha}}
+    end
+  end
+
+  defp validate_pull_request(pull_request, identity) do
+    cond do
+      pull_request["headSha"] != identity.head_sha -> {:error, :stale_pull_request_head}
+      pull_request["state"] != "open" -> {:error, :pull_request_not_open}
+      not is_boolean(pull_request["draft"]) -> {:error, :pull_request_draft_state_missing}
+      true -> :ok
+    end
+  end
+
+  defp promote_and_confirm(client, issue, identity) do
+    mutation_result = client.mark_pull_request_ready(issue, identity.number, identity.head_sha)
+    repository_id = issue.native_ref["repositoryId"]
+
+    case client.fetch_pull_request(repository_id, identity.number) do
+      {:ok, pull_request} -> confirm_ready_after_mutation(pull_request, identity, mutation_result)
+      {:error, reason} -> {:error, {:pull_request_readiness_confirmation_failed, reason}}
+    end
+  end
+
+  defp confirm_ready_after_mutation(pull_request, identity, mutation_result) do
+    with :ok <- validate_pull_request(pull_request, identity) do
+      cond do
+        pull_request["draft"] == false -> :ok
+        match?({:error, _}, mutation_result) -> mutation_result
+        true -> {:error, :pull_request_readiness_not_confirmed}
+      end
+    end
+  end
+
   defp run_verification_turn(app_server, workspace, issue, recipient, worker_host) do
     prompt = """
     You are the BOS post-approval deployment verifier for #{issue.identifier}.
-    The human Approval is durable. Re-read the exact PR HEAD, approval and
-    evidence through bos-mcp. Request the policy-checked merge; never force it.
+    The human Approval and exact-HEAD ready-for-review transition are durable.
+    Re-read the exact PR HEAD, ready state, approval and evidence through
+    bos-mcp. Request the policy-checked merge; never force it.
     Perform the repository's real deployment or rollback workflow for that exact
     commit, create the GitHub Deployment through bos-mcp, and invoke the matching
     Vercel or VPS provider inspection. Against the real external URL, execute the
diff --git a/elixir/test/symphony_elixir/post_approval_coordinator_test.exs b/elixir/test/symphony_elixir/post_approval_coordinator_test.exs
index fdc2e501ab72d9094f1534cd195ea93793284d27..1deb050d6323e4fe6ebf6f699a4f8567341a6e35
--- a/elixir/test/symphony_elixir/post_approval_coordinator_test.exs
+++ b/elixir/test/symphony_elixir/post_approval_coordinator_test.exs
@@ -47,9 +47,40 @@
     def fetch_issue("bos-front", 42), do: {:ok, %{"labels" => ["bos:issue", "agent:done"]}}
 
     def fetch_run("bos-front", "run_001") do
-      {:ok, %{"status" => "completed", "currentAttemptId" => "attempt_002"}}
+      {:ok,
+       %{
+         "status" => "completed",
+         "currentAttemptId" => "attempt_002",
+         "pullRequestNumber" => 17,
+         "headCommit" => "a1b2c3d4"
+       }}
     end
 
+    def fetch_pull_request("bos-front", 17) do
+      state = Agent.get(Application.fetch_env!(:symphony_elixir, :post_approval_pr_state), & &1)
+
+      {:ok,
+       %{
+         "number" => 17,
+         "headSha" => state.head_sha,
+         "state" => state.pr_state,
+         "draft" => state.draft
+       }}
+    end
+
+    def mark_pull_request_ready(_issue, 17, "a1b2c3d4") do
+      agent = Application.fetch_env!(:symphony_elixir, :post_approval_pr_state)
+
+      result =
+        Agent.get_and_update(agent, fn state ->
+          next = %{state | draft: false, promotions: state.promotions + 1}
+          {state.mutation_result, next}
+        end)
+
+      send(Application.fetch_env!(:symphony_elixir, :post_approval_test_pid), :promoted)
+      result
+    end
+
     def fetch_run_artifacts("bos-front", "run_001", "deployment-verifications") do
       {:ok,
        [
@@ -78,10 +109,30 @@
     end
   end
 
+  defmodule RejectingClient do
+    defdelegate fetch_run(repository_id, run_id), to: FakeClient
+    defdelegate fetch_pull_request(repository_id, number), to: FakeClient
+
+    def mark_pull_request_ready(_issue, 17, "a1b2c3d4"),
+      do: {:error, {:game_api_http_error, 503}}
+  end
+
+  setup do
+    Application.put_env(:symphony_e

commandActions.command: mise exec -- mix format lib/symphony_elixir/game_api/client.ex lib/symphony_elixir/post_approval_coordinator.ex test/symphony_elixir/post_approval_coordinator_test.exs && mise exec -- mix test test/symphony_elixir/post_approval_coordinator_test.exs && mise exec -- mix specs.check

item.text: The focused lifecycle suite now passes all five cases: normal promotion, response-loss restart recovery with one mutation, stale HEAD blocking, closed PR blocking, and provider failure preservation. Public-function spec validation is also clean; I’m moving to the repository-wide required test gate now.

summary: Codex command started: /bin/bash -lc 'mise exec -- mix test'
